title: Subsea electric actuator
abstract: A subsea electric actuator includes a housing configured to be filled with dielectric oil, an electric motor, a brake, a clutch, and a spring. The clutch and the brake are configured to maintain the spring in a compressed position. In certain embodiments, the actuator further includes a planetary gear and a multi-turn gear assembly, such that the planetary gear and the multi-turn gear assembly are configured to multiply the torque provided by the electric motor. The actuator can further include a ball screw and a nut screw configured to convert the torque provided by the motor into linear thrust to compress the spring.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A subsea electric actuator comprising: (a) a housing configured to be filled with dielectric oil; (b) an electric motor; (c) a brake; (d) a clutch; and (e) a spring, wherein said clutch and said brake are configured to maintain said spring in a compressed position. 2. The subsea electric actuator of claim 1, wherein said housing is separated into a first cavity and a second cavity. 3. The subsea electric actuator of claim 1, wherein said housing made of steel. 4. The subsea electric actuator of claim 1, further comprising: (f) a planetary gear; and (g) a multi-turn gear assembly, wherein said planetary gear and said multi-turn gear assembly are configured to multiply the torque provided by said electric motor. 5. The subsea electric actuator of claim 4, further comprising: (h) a ball screw; and (i) a nut screw, 

 wherein said ball screw and said nut screw are configured to convert the torque provided by said motor into linear thrust to compress said spring. 6. The subsea electric actuator of claim 5, further comprising: (j) a damper configured to regulate the closing speed of a valve. 7. The subsea electric actuator of claim 6, wherein said damper is configured to displace said dielectric oil to a flow regulating orifice. 8. The subsea electric actuator of claim 5, further comprising: (j) a CPU configured to monitor an at least one aspect of said subsea electric actuator. 9. The subsea electric actuator of claim 8, further comprising: (k) a position indicator sensor configured to verify the position of a valve. 10. The subsea electric actuator of claim 8, further comprising: (k) a water presence sensor for monitoring the presence of water in said dielectric oil. 11. The subsea electric actuator of claim 8, further comprising: (k) a load cell. 12. The subsea electric actuator of claim 11, wherein said load cell comprises a plurality of strain gages configured to monitor the performance of said subsea electric actuator. 13. The subsea electric actuator of claim 8, further comprising: (k) a motor driver. 14. The subsea electric actuator of claim 8, further comprising: (k) a compensator configured to balance the internal pressure of said subsea electric actuator with an exterior pressure. 15. The subsea electric actuator of claim 8, further comprising: (k) a shock absorber. 16. The subsea electric actuator of claim 8, further comprising: (k) a safety valve configured to discharge overpressure in said housing to an external environment. 17. The subsea electric actuator of claim 8, further comprising: (k) a position indicator configured to display via a physical indication the position of a valve. 18. The subsea electric actuator of claim 1, wherein said clutch can be engaged to move a valve by said motor if said spring fails. 19. The subsea electric actuator of claim 8, wherein said CPU is configured to monitor one of dynamic thrust, speed, position, and acceleration of the actuator stroking output. 20. The subsea electric actuator of claim 8, wherein said subsea electric actuator is configured to be able to undergo a partial stroking test in order to verify that said spring is operating properly.
